# SendFast — Complete Reference

> End-to-end encrypted file transfer. Zero-knowledge. No account required.

## Overview

SendFast lets users send files of any type with client-side AES-256-GCM encryption. Files are encrypted in the browser before upload. The encryption key is embedded in the URL fragment (#key=...) and never transmitted to SendFast servers, ensuring true zero-knowledge architecture.

## How Encryption Works

1. User selects files in the browser
2. Browser generates a random AES-256-GCM encryption key
3. Each file is encrypted client-side using the Web Crypto API
4. Encrypted files are uploaded to storage
5. A shareable URL is generated: https://sendfast.app/transfer/{id}#{encryption_key}
6. The encryption key is in the URL fragment — it is never sent to the server
7. Recipient opens the link, browser extracts key from the fragment
8. Encrypted files are downloaded and decrypted client-side
9. The server never sees unencrypted data or the encryption key

## Pricing

### Free Plan ($0/forever)
- 2 GB maximum per transfer
- 7-day link expiry
- 10 transfers per month
- End-to-end encryption
- Shareable download links
- No account required

### Starter Plan ($9/month)
- 25 GB maximum per transfer
- 30-day link expiry
- 100 transfers per month
- Everything in Free, plus:
- Password protection
- Email delivery to recipients
- Download tracking and analytics
- Priority support

### Pro Plan ($19/month)
- 100 GB maximum per transfer
- 90-day link expiry
- 500 transfers per month
- Everything in Starter, plus:
- REST API access
- Custom branding on download pages
- Receive links (collect files from others)

## REST API (Pro Plan)

Base URL: https://sendfast.app/api

### Upload Files
```
POST /api/upload
Content-Type: multipart/form-data

Parameters:
- files: File[] (encrypted file blobs)
- ivs: string[] (initialization vectors, base64)
- sizes: number[] (original file sizes in bytes)
- types: string[] (MIME types)
- expiry: "1h" | "24h" | "7d" | "30d" | "90d"
- password?: string (optional password protection)
- emails?: string (comma-separated recipient emails)
- title?: string (transfer title)
- message?: string (message for recipients)

Response: { transferId: string }
```

### Get Transfer Info
```
GET /api/download/{transferId}
Query: ?password={password} (if password-protected)

Response: {
  id: string,
  title: string | null,
  message: string | null,
  files: [{ id, name, size, type, iv, url }],
  expiresAt: string | null,
  downloadCount: number,
  passwordProtected: boolean,
  createdAt: string
}
```

### List Transfers (authenticated)
```
GET /api/transfers
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {session_token}

Response: Transfer[]
```

### Delete Transfer (authenticated)
```
DELETE /api/transfers/{id}
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {session_token}

Response: { success: boolean }
```

## Agent Integration Guide

To use SendFast programmatically as an AI agent:

1. **Obtain a Pro account** for API access
2. **Encrypt files client-side** using AES-256-GCM (Web Crypto API or equivalent)
3. **Upload encrypted blobs** via POST /api/upload
4. **Store the encryption key** — it goes in the URL fragment, not sent to the server
5. **Share the full URL** (including #fragment) with the intended recipient
6. **Track downloads** via GET /api/download/{id}

Important: The encryption key must NEVER be sent to the server. It must only be transmitted to the recipient via the URL fragment or a secure side channel.

## Tech Stack

- Next.js 15 (App Router)
- TypeScript (strict mode)
- PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- S3-compatible object storage (MinIO)
- AES-256-GCM encryption (Web Crypto API)
- Stripe for payments
- Better Auth for authentication

## Contact

Website: https://sendfast.app
Support: support@sendfast.app
